Convert column filters to client-side with adjusted pagination

while a filter is active. Filters reset on folder change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(async): promote await_holding_lock to deny (ASYNC-05) (#690)

## Summary

- Promote `clippy::await_holding_lock` from `warn` to `deny` in
workspace `Cargo.toml`
- Remove the redundant `warn` override from `tui/Cargo.toml` so the
workspace `deny` propagates uniformly
- Add explanatory comment: *"Correctness: holding std::sync::Mutex
across .await causes deadlocks"*

## Audit findings

Full audit of every `Mutex::lock()` call site — zero violations found:

| Location | Lock type | Pattern | Safe? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `agora/semeion` | `tokio::sync::Mutex` | `.lock().await` with scoped
guards | ✅ |
| `nous/actor`, `pipeline`, `adapters` | `tokio::sync::Mutex` |
`.lock().await` properly scoped | ✅ |
| `nous/manager` | `std::sync::Mutex` (join handle) | Guard dropped at
statement end, `.await` is after | ✅ |
| `hermeneus/health` | `std::sync::Mutex` | Sync-only struct methods, no
async context | ✅ |
| `organon` builtins | `std::sync::Mutex` | Test mock structs, sync
context only | ✅ |
| `mneme/engine` | `std::sync::Mutex` | Vendored CozoDB runtime — no
`await_holding_lock` suppressions | ✅ |
| `melete/distill` | `std::sync::Mutex` | Test structs, sync context | ✅
|

## Mneme engine note

`grep -rn 'await_holding_lock\|expect.*clippy' crates/mneme/src/engine/`
returned no results — the vendored engine has no suppressions for this
lint.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` — zero
warnings/errors
- [x] `cargo test --workspace` — all tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Cody Kickertz <cody@forkwright.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: pluggable encryption layer (age, GPG, AES-256-GCM)

Add encrypt-at-rest between engine dump and store upload, following
the existing plugin architecture (ABC + factory + registry).

Backends: age (recommended, subprocess), GPG (subprocess), and
AES-256-GCM (pure Python, chunked for large files). Encryption
profiles are defined in config and referenced by jobs.

Pipeline: dump → compress → encrypt → SHA256 → upload
Restore: download → SHA256 check → decrypt → verify → restore

feat(analytics): smart points input with live EUR valuation

Replace EUR commission input with MR/miles points input. The system
detects point type from product (regular Amex = MR, FB Amex = miles),
shows the MR-to-miles conversion (5:4 ratio), and calculates EUR value
live from the user's target_cpm setting. Changing mile valuation in
the app automatically updates all conversion values.

- Add referral_points column to cms_affiliate_conversions
- Read user's target_cpm from profiles table
- Auto-detect MR vs miles from product_key prefix
- Show live conversion: MR -> miles -> EUR in modal
- Dashboard KPIs and logs show points, miles, and live EUR value
- Per-product breakdown shows MR/miles totals

commit.message:
feat(tasks): smart dispatch form + stop task button

Dispatch form:
- Replace raw JSON textarea with per-field inputs (label + placeholder)
- Fields are fully dynamic — change agent or task type and the form
  updates instantly with the exact fields that agent expects
- Text, number, and textarea inputs based on field type
- Required fields marked with * and enforced before submit

Stop task:
- Add POST /tasks/:id/cancel orchestrator endpoint — marks pending/
  running tasks as failed ("Cancelled by user") and broadcasts WS event
- Add api.cancel() in dashboard api.ts
- Show X stop button on every pending/running task row; hides for
  completed/failed tasks; optimistic UI update on success

Repository Consolidation - Remove 189 redundant files (#16)

* [RELEASE] v4.6.3

Fix Console Server asset serving and workspace root resolution.

1. express.static missing dotfiles:"allow" — all CSS/JS/images silently
   404'd under dotfile install paths (.vscode/, .antigravity/).
2. getWorkspaceRoot() used process.cwd() instead of this.workspaceRoot,
   causing bootstrap detection and workspace-scoped reads to check the
   wrong directory.

* [RELEASE] v4.6.4

Governance state integrity remediation. Trust data that was previously
transient or fabricated is now persisted, verified, and kept in sync.

- TrustEngine split into 3 files for Section 4 compliance (449L → 223+167+40)
- Event-driven cache invalidation via EventBus (3 new trust event types)
- Optimistic locking with version-based concurrency control and retry
- Checkpoint chain auto-verified on startup (was assumed valid)
- Trust timestamps use DB updated_at (was fabricated new Date())
- Command Center version reads from package.json (was hardcoded 4.4.0)
- 15 new TDD-Light tests (trust-calculator, trust-persistence)

* fix: Command Center state management and Gemini Nano detection

Phase 1: Tab switch state caching
- Add lastHubData cache to ConnectionClient
- Use cached data on tab switch instead of empty object

Phase 2: Scaffold callback initialization
- Add setScaffoldCallback() in bootstrapServers.ts
- Implement full skill scaffolding logic

Phase 3: Phase title/index synchronization
- Fix getPhaseInfo() to use consistent defaults

Phase 4: Voice feature feedback
- Add error feedback in wake-word-listener.js
- Add model load feedback in stt-engine.js
- Enhance wireModelProgress() in voice-controller.js

Phase 5: Gemini Nano detection
- Track nativeUnavailableReason in web-llm-engine.js

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Performance: Stream JSON during import for memory improvements (#12276)

* Perf: stream manifest parsing with ijson in document_importer

Replace bulk json.load of the full manifest (which materializes the
entire JSON array into memory) with incremental ijson streaming.
Eliminates self.manifest entirely — records are never all in memory
at once.

- Add ijson>=3.2 dependency
- New module-level iter_manifest_records() generator
- load_manifest_files() collects paths only; no parsing at load time
- check_manifest_validity() streams without accumulating records
- decrypt_secret_fields() streams each manifest to a .decrypted.json
  temp file record-by-record; temp files cleaned up after file copy
- _import_files_from_manifest() collects only document records (small
  fraction of manifest) for the tqdm progress bar

Measured on 200 docs + 200 CustomFieldInstances:
- Streaming validation: peak memory 3081 KiB -> 333 KiB (89% reduction)
- Stream-decrypt to file: peak memory 3081 KiB -> 549 KiB (82% reduction)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Perf: slim dict in _import_files_from_manifest, discard fields

When collecting document records for the file-copy step, extract only
the 4 keys the loop actually uses (pk + 3 exported filename keys) and
discard the full fields dict (content, checksum, tags, etc.).

Peak memory for the document-record list: 939 KiB -> 375 KiB (60% reduction).
Wall time unchanged.

Add Windows-portable MATLAB Function model (enginespeed_qat_mlfcn.slx)

- enginespeed_qat_mlfcn.slx: cross-platform closed-loop ROM model using
  a MATLAB Function block instead of MEX S-Function. QAT LSTM-32 weights
  (int8 dequantized) embedded directly in MATLAB code; no compilation needed.
  Runs on Windows, Linux, and macOS with any MATLAB + Simulink installation.

- scripts/create_mlfcn_model.m: builds the MLFCN model programmatically.
  Reads weights from rom_lstm_qat.c, writes full LSTM forward pass with
  persistent h/c state via Stateflow EMChart API.

- scripts/build_sfun_windows.m: alternative cross-platform MEX compile
  script for users who want native MEX performance. Auto-detects platform
  via mexext() and compiles sfun_rom_lstm_qat.c accordingly.

- index.html: added mlfcn model card (Windows-Ready badge), 2 new script
  cards in Phase 4/5 group, MATLAB Scripts badge 9→11.

commit.message:
Add requestContext snapshot to tracing spans (#14020)

## Description

This PR adds automatic capture of `RequestContext` snapshots to tracing
spans. Each span now stores a serialized snapshot of the active
`RequestContext` at the time of span creation, making request-scoped
values (user IDs, tenant IDs, feature flags, etc.) available when
viewing traces.

### Key Changes

- **Span Enhancement**: Added `requestContext` field to `BaseSpan` that
captures a snapshot of the `RequestContext` when a span is created
- **Serialization**: Request context values are serialized using
`deepClean` to handle non-serializable values (functions are replaced
with `'[Function]'`)
- **Empty Context Handling**: Empty request contexts are not stored
(undefined if no data)
- **Child Span Support**: Child spans can optionally capture their own
request context snapshots, allowing different context states at
different span levels
- **Database Schema Updates**: Added `requestContext` column to spans
table across all storage backends (ClickHouse, LibSQL, MSSQL,
PostgreSQL) with backwards compatibility via `alterTable` with
`ifNotExists`
- **Exporter Updates**: Updated exporters (Cloud, Default) to include
`requestContext` in exported span records
- **Type Updates**: Updated TypeScript interfaces and Zod schemas to
include the new field

### Testing

Added comprehensive test coverage including:
- Root span request context capture
- Exported span serialization
- Child span context snapshots (with independent state capture)
- Child spans without explicit context
- Empty context handling
- Non-serializable value filtering

Integration tests verify request context propagation through agent
generation with multiple span types.

## Type of Change

- [x] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [x] Documentation update
- [x] Test update

## Checklist

- [x] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [x] I have added tests that prove my feature works
- [x] All existing tests pass

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QaSLa999aBbRDenrGqtgic

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

## Release Notes

* **New Features**
* Request context snapshot is now captured in tracing spans, making
request-scoped values (user IDs, tenant IDs, feature flags) available
for enhanced observability.

* **Documentation**
  * Updated tracing spans reference documentation.

* **Tests**
* Added comprehensive tests verifying request context propagation and
serialization across traces.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement observability contract validation with fail-closed semantics (RGC-066A)

Expands the observability channel model with a comprehensive contract validation
framework that enforces fail-closed invariants on engine telemetry surfaces:

- New ObservabilityContractViolation struct for explicit violation reporting
- New validate_observability_contract() function checking:
  • Redaction-before-sampling requirement (privacy guarantee)
  • Family overlap detection between lossless and approximate surfaces
  • Operator mode contract uniqueness (no ambiguous telemetry paths)
  • Sampling determinism requirements for reproducibility
  • Support bundle export rules (reject lossy export paths)
  • Sketch bound calibration constraints
- Machine-readable contract schema (docs/rgc_observability_channel_policy_v1.json)
- Operator verification documentation (docs/RGC_OBSERVABILITY_CHANNEL_POLICY_V1.md)
- Shell script for running the observability validation gate
- Integration tests: canonical contract validation, rejection of missing
  sampling determinism, rejection of support bundle downsampling

  - POST /batch — lists all SOPs/procedures available for scripting
  - POST /download/:documentId — returns script as downloadable .txt file
- Scripts written as Max's voice — conversational, mentor-style narration
- Targets 5-10 scenes per video, 15-30 seconds each (2-4 min total)
- Supports EN/FR/ES script generation

Frontend:
- Added "Generate Video Script" button to DocViewer footer
- Script panel slides in below document content with scene count/duration
- Copy-to-clipboard button for pasting directly into Synthesia
- Show/Hide toggle after initial generation
- Passes language prop for multilingual script generation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Standardise example filenames and tighten .gitignore

- All examples now use example-[description] prefix with consistent dashes
- Add .gitignore patterns to prevent future exported JSONs landing in examples/

commit.message:
Add entropic policy morphing, escape analysis certificates, and hostcall session protocol/transport modules

Introduces four new RGC-track feature modules with full integration test coverage:

- Entropic Policy Morphing (RGC-617B): Policy transition-budget control between
  detected regimes using fixed-point entropy bounds and epoch-based adaptation.
  Falls back to anchor policy when budget exhausted or safety invariants fail.

- Escape Analysis Certificates (RGC-622A): 4-level escape state lattice
  (NoEscape → ArgEscape → ThreadEscape → GlobalEscape) with alias classes,
  liveness envelopes, and optimization eligibility certificates. Self-contained
  certificates include inputs, derived facts, and invalidation reasons.

- Hostcall Session Protocol (RGC-505A): Typestate session protocol with
  compile-time-checked phase transitions (Uninit → Negotiating → Established →
  Closing → Closed), key schedule derivation, persistent anti-replay ledger,
  and degraded-mode semantics for partial authentication failures.

- Hostcall Batch Transport (RGC-505B): Batched zero-copy hostcall transport
  with credit-based flow control, backpressure, shared memory region descriptors,
  and safety membrane validation. Builds on the session protocol formal definitions.

Each module includes comprehensive integration tests covering corpus invariants,
serde round-trips, typestate machines, and protocol validation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
